title: The weighted two-cell average preserves a constant field

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the total mass m0+m1 is nonzero, the weighted average of two equal cell values u is u.
assumptions: m0, m1, and u are real numbers
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted average of a constant two-cell field equals that constant.

declarations.name: weightedCellAverage2
declarations.statement: def weightedCellAverage2 (m0 m1 u0 u1 : Real) : Real := (m0 * u0 + m1 * u1) / (m0 + m1)

declarations.name: weightedCellAverage2_const
declarations.statement: theorem weightedCellAverage2_const (m0 m1 u : Real) (hsum : m0 + m1 ≠ 0) : weightedCellAverage2 m0 m1 u u = u

tasks.input: Translate constant preservation for a nondegenerate weighted two-cell average into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedCellAverage2_const (m0 m1 u : Real) (hsum : m0 + m1 ≠ 0) : weightedCellAverage2 m0 m1 u u = u

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding the weighted average and simplifying the nonzero denominator.
tasks.target: Unfold weightedCellAverage2 and use field_simp with hsum.
